ground_truth.title: Best Practices and Expectations for Effective Course Delivery | Faculty Development
ground_truth.main_content: The online learning environment presents a unique set of challenges that requires a clear definition of instructor performance. The following best practices identify the minimum level of interaction and management required of an instructor, to maintain a quality online learning environment. As a course instructor, it is anticipated that you will follow the established course start and end dates, with schedule adjustments as needed for graduating students and others with unique circumstances. You should follow consistent guidelines for managing your course, with specific tasks performed daily, weekly, or each semester. Being consistently present for your students is the key to effectively managing any online course. Regular contact with your students helps assure them that they are well supported. The online classroom can become even more personal than a class that meets face-to-face. You should monitor assignment submissions and communication with students to remind them of missed and upcoming deadlines. Good practice suggests posting a message or announcement to your class at least once a week to tell students what you will cover in the coming week and remind them of any due dates. Early in the course, you should establish a regular schedule for when you will be logging in to the course and communicate this to students, typically once per day. Many World Campus students are adult learners who have work and family responsibilities and tend to be more active on weekends. If you are unable to log into Canvas for several days or more, give prior notice to students and the administrative unit overseeing your course. Providing this information upfront will help to forestall many student inquiries. You should provide feedback to student inquiries within one business day and be accessible. Because online learners must manage their time carefully, timely instructor feedback is especially important to them. Without it, they may not make progress on their coursework. You should provide timely and meaningful feedback on student work using clear and concise language. Students need specific information about what made the work good and what needs more work and how they can improve. Let students know how quickly you will grade and return all assignments and exams. Students need to know when to expect feedback to manage their time and learning. Please strive to grade assignments, including meaningful feedback, as promptly as possible. As a rule, it is reasonable to expect that students will receive their grades, including feedback, within one week of submitting an assignment. You should provide a teaching and learning environment that supports academic freedom. Faculty are free to discuss their subjects in the online classroom and students must also be free to express their opinions without fear of ridicule, intimidation, or retaliation. You should make sure you have immediate and predictable access to the same technology that is required for students in your course. You should post final course grades to LionPATH on time, within two business days of the course end date. You should encourage students to complete the University's end-of-course Student Educational Experience Questionnaire (SEEQ). Research has shown that the instructor has the most influence on whether a student completes an end-of-course survey. You should contribute to the continual updating and improvement of the course content. Keep the course current and routinely tweak the course based on feedback from students, peers, and administrators.
